questions.Q1_Coverage_Gap: Entry points with zero validated/needs-deeper hypotheses: DestinationResource.get, DestinationResource.delete, AlertResource.get/post/delete, AlertMuteResource.post/delete, AlertSubscriptionListResource.get, AlertSubscriptionResource.delete, DestinationTypeListResource.get, AlertListResource.get, EventsResource.get.
questions.Q2_Chain_Seeding: Chain seeds: PH-02 (non-admin destination subscription) can chain into higher-severity SSRF outcomes if admin-configured destinations target sensitive internal services; PH-03/PH-01 (destination SSRF) can chain into internal control-plane actions depending on target service; PH-03 (event webhook forwarding) can chain into internal service actions if hooks target privileged endpoints; PH-05 (event ingestion exhaustion) can chain into alert delivery disruption and monitoring blind spots.
questions.Q3_Fragile_Safety: PH-01 (Round 1) invalidated with Fragile score — payload envelope blocks Docker API body shape but any envelope change could reopen; candidate for re-investigation if envelope changes or if internal endpoint accepts schema wrapper.
questions.Q4_Model_Coverage: Untouched entry points: DestinationResource.get/delete, DestinationTypeListResource.get, AlertResource.* (get/post/delete), AlertMuteResource.*, AlertSubscriptionListResource.get, AlertSubscriptionResource.delete, AlertListResource.get, EventsResource.get. Trust chain gaps not directly addressed: outbound HTTP for non-webhook destinations (Slack/Discord/etc) still uses raw requests.post but shares same SSRF class already covered.
questions.Q5_Impact_Multiplication: No NEEDS-DEEPER items; repeated alert evaluation (PH-04/PH-02) can amplify SSRF impact of PH-01/PH-03 and turn medium into high via DoS or internal rate exhaustion if validated in deployment.
questions.Decision: Run another loop (coverage gaps present and Fragile invalidation exists).

questions.Q1_Coverage_Gap: Entry points with zero validated/needs-deeper hypotheses: DestinationResource.get, DestinationResource.delete, DestinationTypeListResource.get, AlertResource.get/post/delete, AlertMuteResource.post/delete, AlertSubscriptionListResource.get, AlertSubscriptionResource.delete, AlertListResource.get, EventsResource.get. (All were analyzed; validated items now focus on audit/logging, PII exposure, and access-control gaps within these endpoints.)
questions.Q2_Chain_Seeding: Chain seeds: PH-01/PH-15 (alert rebind to unauthorized query) can chain into data exfil via webhook destinations; PH-05 (event webhook amplification) can chain into internal service disruption if hooks target internal endpoints; PH-12/PH-06 (event webhook envelope forwarding) can chain into internal automation endpoints; PH-03/PH-14 (subscriber PII exposure) can chain into targeted phishing leading to account compromise.
questions.Q3_Fragile_Safety: Fragile invalidations: PH-01/02/03/04/05/06/07/09/10/11 (loop2 Round-1) rely on single auth/permission checks; PH-16 (unauthenticated /api/events) blocked only by login_required; PH-17 (URL masking) depends on schema secret list. These are fragility notes but not open findings.
questions.Q4_Model_Coverage: All entry points were covered in loop 2; remaining issues are mostly design/authorization expectations (admin-only access, audit logging). Trust chain gaps addressed: event webhook forwarding and destination SSRF, plus alert update access checks.
questions.Q5_Impact_Multiplication: No NEEDS-DEEPER items; validated alert rebind (PH-15) increases severity of webhook SSRF by allowing unauthorized query data exposure; alert evaluation spam (PH-13) amplifies SSRF/DoS against webhook targets.
questions.Decision: Proceed to summary (no NEEDS-DEEPER and coverage gaps addressed).
